---
title: "progress-report-sebanara215"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-29"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(rvest)
library(Lahman)
library(tidyverse)
head(HallOfFame, 3)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

##Clean dataset

```{r}
library(rvest)
library(tidyverse)
library(Lahman)

url <- "https://www.baseball-reference.com/awards/hof_2026.shtml"

page <- read_html(url)

hof_node <- page %>%
  html_element("#hof_BBWAA")

col_names <- hof_node %>%
  html_elements("thead tr") %>%
  .[[2]] %>%
  html_elements("th") %>%
  html_text()

hof_raw <- hof_node %>%
  html_table(fill = TRUE)

names(hof_raw) <- make.names(col_names, unique = TRUE)

names(hof_raw)

hof_clean <- hof_raw %>%
  
  mutate(
    Votes = as.numeric(Votes),
    
    percent_vote = as.numeric(
      str_remove(X.vote, "%")
    ),
    
    year = 2026,
    
    inducted = if_else(percent_vote >= 75, "Y", "N")
  ) %>%
  
  select(
    candidate = Name,
    year,
    votes = Votes,
    percent_vote,
    inducted
  )

hof_clean

write_csv(hof_clean, "hof_2026_clean.csv")

HallOfFame %>%
  filter(yearID == 2026)

HallOfFame %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = yearID, fill = inducted)) +
  geom_bar() +
  xlim(c(1936, 2026))
```
